# FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

## Aura: A Sovereign, Military-Grade Decentralized AI Monorepo Launched by Yani Meziani

**[CITY, STATE] — [DATE]** — Today, Yani Meziani (ORCID: 0009-0007-4348-8711) announced the open-source launch of **Aura**, a "Sovereign Agentic Monorepo" designed to put control of AI research and deployment back into the hands of individual operators. Building on the foundational work of the **Akasha 2** paper (arXiv:2601.06212), Aura offers a **military-grade decentralized** alternative to centralized AI platforms.

"As AI becomes more integrated into our lives, the need for sovereign, local-first solutions becomes critical," said Yani Meziani. "Aura is designed to be a 'pocket business'—a complete, military-grade ecosystem for managing AI agents safely, whether you're a solo researcher or a tech-forward small business."

### Key Features of Aura:
- **Military-Grade Decentralization**: Utilizing zero-trust security protocols and Hamiltonian State Space Duality (HSSD) for resilient agent coordination.
- **Mobile Control (Pegasus)**: Full mission control from an Android device, featuring Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) approvals for sensitive agent actions.
- **Lean Runtime (Cerberus)**: A high-performance Zig-based engine for hosting autonomous agents with minimal overhead.
- **Continuous Publication**: A built-in pipeline for automatically preparing research and manifestos for submission to arXiv.

Aura is now available on GitHub under the MIT License, inviting developers and researchers to contribute to the future of sovereign AI.

### About Yani Meziani
Yani Meziani is an AI researcher and developer focused on building resilient, human-centric AI systems. His recent work on **Akasha 2** has introduced significant advancements in multimodal architectures and Hamiltonian State Space Duality.
